ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ Ebɔrɛ nɛfa bumo nseŋ kaŋɛ bumo le: “Men kurge n wora keshi n salga to n sɔ kasawule so kikɛ nsaa ji eleŋ kumo so. Men baa ji eleŋ ekɔrɔtɔ so nɛ mbuibi so nɛ asɔ gbeyeso kikɛ nyam so.”
translated: The old man said to the young man: "I will tell you a story about a hunter who went to the bush and found a leopard cub. He took the cub home and raised it. He fed it with milk and meat. The leopard grew up and became very strong."

ref: Forty days later Noah opened a window to send out a raven, but it kept flying around until the water had dried up.
text: nseŋ lara nyiŋgbeŋ ashi kuloŋgboŋ na to n lɛ kowu nɛ k baa wɔtɔ a lɛa a lɛa a jo saŋɛ nɛ nchu na kikɛ nyam beeŋ feeto kasawule so kuraa.
translated: The old woman said that she would go and buy some food for the children because they were hungry.

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: N wu fanɛ ma asheŋ bɔlɔ fo kenishi, ŋkpal manɛ so, fo wora ma kelela ga m mɔlga ma ŋkpa, ama abee na ase wɔ kufɔ ga. Amoso pɔɛŋ nɛ n fo ndoŋ kusɔ ko beeŋ tiŋ n wora ma nɛ n wu.
translated: When you are going to the farm, take your cutlass, and if you meet a wild animal, don't be afraid, just cut it down. If you don't kill it, it will kill you.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Kumo be kaman nɛ e kaŋɛ anya na to be emo nɛ e daŋ juŋkpar na fanɛ mo da Isɔɔ baŋ tu mo nseŋ bishi mo fanɛ, “Wanɛ be esa e la fo? Nnɛ nɛ fee yɔ? Wanɛ e wɔ asɔbɔya damta nɛ b juŋkpar fo ere kikɛ?”
translated: A man was walking along the road when he saw a woman sitting by the roadside. He asked her, "What is your name? Where are you going? Why are you sitting here alone?"

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
